A ladies' auxiliary is to be estab-
lished in connection with the West
ern Federation of Miners. 1t is ex-
pected that this move will rebound
greatly to the progress of the min
ers' organization.


Clarence Smith writes that there
Is not a spark of bitterness in the
American Labor union against the
membership of the American Fed
GRAFTON of Labor. There are Just
rs good and true men in the rank
and file of one organization as in
the other"


William Potter of Schenectady.
who recently secured an injunction
against President Schaefer of the
painters union. compelling him to
reinstate him as a member. from
which he was ousted on account
of his membership in the national
guard. was yesterday temporarily
reinStated.


Iron Moulders' union, No. 33S,
met last evening at musicians
room, Central Labor hall No bust
ness of any Importance was trans
acted except the installation of one
officer, J. c. Robertson, who was
elected to the position of CORE
spending representative The Union
Park foundry, more commonly
known as the Spokane foundry,
was put on tho fair list. The iron
moulders claim to be tho oldest
national organization in the coun-
try having been first organized in
1857.


A large number of delegates at
tended the meeting of the trades
council held last evening. The
meeting lasted for a longer time
than usual.


The district council of the car
penters' union will meet tonight in
the musicians room. Central Labor
hall. This council Is composed OF
delegates from the two local car.
